rulings.Case Title: CERTAIN UNDERWRITERS AT LLOYD'S LONDON ET AL VS. CONNECT ART INTRNATIONAL, LLC ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Attorneys Fees, Et Al.
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Monday, November 4, 2019, Line 4, DEFENDANT CONNECT ART INTRNATIONAL, LLC's Motion For Attorneys Fees. Defendant Connect Art International, LLC's motion for attorney's fees against Plaintiffs is denied. Assuming that the bills of lading are binding on Plaintiffs, the provision under which Defendant seeks to recover its attorney's fees is a third-party claims indemnity provision, not a prevailing party attorney's fees clause. By that provision, entitled "WAIVER OF SUBROGATION," "the Shipper agrees to indemnify and hold harmless CAI from any claim or lawsuit arising from the shipment of the Property via CAI, including but not limited to, any action commenced by any insurers or third parties." "A clause which contains the words 'indemnify' and 'hold harmless' is an indemnity clause which generally obligates the indemnitor to reimburse the indemnitee for any damages the indemnitee becomes obligated to pay third persons." (Myers Bldg. Industries, Ltd. v. Interface Technology, Inc. (1993) 13 Cal.App.4th 949, 969 [holding that award of attorney's fees in breach of contract action was not supported by third-party claims indemnity clauses which did not provide for award of attorney's fees incurred in actions to enforce contract]. "Courts will not infer the parties intended an indemnification provision to cover attorney fees between the parties if the provision 'does not specifically provide for attorney's fees in an action on the contract.'" (Alki Partners, LP v. DB Funds Services, LLC (2016) 4 Cal.App.5th 574, 604 [contractual indemnity provision did not grant a right to recover attorney fees as a prevailing party, where provision did not contain the phrase "prevailing party" or refer to an action between the parties for breach of the agreement]; accord, Carr Business Enterprises, Inc. v. City of Chowchilla (2008) 166 Cal.App.4th 14, 23 [indemnity provision would not support attorney's fees award where "there is no express language authorizing recovery of fees in an action to enforce the contract"].) While the provision refers to "any action commenced by any insurers," Lloyd's is a party to this action only in its capacity as a subrogee of the Seidenbergs, and therefore is not a third party within the meaning of this provision. (See, e.g., Fireman's Fund Ins. Co. v. Maryland Casualty Co. (1994) 21 Cal.App.4th 1586, 1596 ["Since the subrogated insurer stands in the shoes of its insured, the insurer has no greater rights against the third party than did the insured"].) Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for the plaintiffs is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EPS)

rulings.Case Title: PHILIP RASKIN VS. MARK LUTZ ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion For Leave To File Third Amended Complaint
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Monday, November 4, 2019, Line 5, PLAINTIFF PHILIP RASKIN's Motion For Leave To File Third Amended Complaint. Plaintiff Philip Raskin's motion for leave to file a third amended complaint adding Curt Welker as an additional plaintiff is granted on the following condition, to which plaintiff must agree either orally at the hearing or in a writing sent to counsel for defendants prior to the hearing. The condition is that the trial is continued to November 2, 2020, with all discovery deadlines triggered off the new trial date. If plaintiff does not agree to this condition, the motion is denied. Granting the motion comports with California's liberal policy of allowing amendments to pleadings. (See, e.g., Howard v. County of San Diego (2010) 184 Cal.App.4th 1422, 1428 ["The policy favoring amendment is so strong that it is a rare case in which denial of leave to amend can be justified."].) The condition on the granting of the motion eliminates any legal prejudice to defendants by the granting of the motion, including their inability prior to the existing trial date to file a timely motion for summary judgment/summary adjudication as to the claims to be newly asserted by Mr. Welker. If defendants wish to challenge those claims, they may do so by way of demurrer to the proposed amended complaint. (See Brown v. Ralphs Grocery Co. (2018) 28 Cal.App.5th 824, 843 ["The 'better course of action' is to allow a plaintiff to amend the complaint 'and then let the parties test its legal sufficiency in other appropriate proceedings.'"].) Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for the plaintiff is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EPS)

rulings.Case Title: JENNIFER SARKANY ET AL VS. CHRISTIE WEST ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Motion For Terminating Sanctions For Witness Intimidation And Harassment Against Plaintiff Abouremeleh Motion For Protective Order For Unwarrented Written Discovery Motion To Disqualify Counsel For Misconduct Request For Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Monday, November 4, 2019, Line 1, DEFENDANTS CHRISTIE WEST, TIMOTHY WEST'S Motion For Terminating Sanctions For Witness Intimidation And Harassment Against Plaintiff Abouremeleh Motion For Protective Order For Unwarrented Written Discovery Motion To Disqualify Counsel For Misconduct Request For Sanctions Pro Tem Judge Scott Borrowman, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: The judge pro tem will email the tentative ruling to the parties because it exceeds the character limits of the tentative ruling system. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to sborrowman@gmail.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: JEREMY FORD VS. MENTORBOX, LLC ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Answers To First Set Of Special Interrogatories And For Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Monday, November 4, 2019, Line 2, DEFENDANT MENTORBOX LLC'S Motion To Compel Answers To First Set Of Special Interrogatories And For Sanctions (Part 1 of 2) Pro Tem Judge Scott Borrowman, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Motion GRANTED. Answers to interrogatories shall be "as complete and straightforward as the information reasonably available to the responding party permits." CCP 2030.220(a). "If only part of an interrogatory is objectionable, the remainder of the interrogatory shall be answered." CCP 2030.240(a). And, if a party objects to "an interrogatory or part of an interrogatory, the specific ground for the objection shall be set forth clearly in the response." CCP 2030.240(b). Boilerplate objections are inappropriate and "may be sanctionable." Korea Data Systems Co. v. Sup. Ct. (1997) 51 Cal.App.4th 1513, 1516. The original answers were boilerplate objections. Because substantive answers were served after the agreed date of 9/13 but before the hearing, I will consider the answers. SI 8, 9: Granted. Plaintiff must describe the questions or state that he cannot comply. CCP 2030.220(a). SI 11, 12, 13, 14: Granted. Interrogatories may call for legal conclusions. CCP 2030.010(b); Rifkind v. Super. Ct. (1994) 22 Cal. App. 4th 1255, 1261. SI 15: Granted. Plaintiff must identify names of persons he remembers. On sanctions: The correspondence does not support Plaintiff's assertion that the parties agreed to stay of discovery to discuss settlement. Banchero Decl. Ex. G disclaims an extension of discovery. Morrison Decl. Ex. B requests Defendant withdraw the motions if it receives "responses without objections." The supplemental answers repeated the boilerplate objections, plus qualified to say that additional responses are "subject to and without waiving the foregoing objections, and to the extent that Plaintiff understands this request," which is neither Code-compliant nor "without objections." Thus, I do not find Plaintiff acted with substantial justification. However, because the motions are "patterned after" each other, the amount of hours appears excessive. Sanctions are GRANTED in the amount of $7,635 (10 hours for Mr. Banchero at $645/hour + 3 hours for Ms. Banchero at $395/hour), payable by 11/29. CCP 2030.300, CRC 3.1348(a). (end of tenative ruling part 1 of 2) =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: JEREMY FORD VS. MENTORBOX, LLC ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Answers To Requests For Admissions And For Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Monday, November 4, 2019, Line 2, DEFENDANT MENTORBOX LLC'S Motion To Compel Answers To Requests For Admissions And For Sanctions Pro Tem Judge Scott Borrowman, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Motion GRANTED in part. Responses to the RFAs were not served by the agreed date of 9/13. The email of 9/18 was a request to extend the deadline but does not operate as a stipulation to extend a deadline that already had passed. However, after the motion was filed but before this hearing, Plaintiff served proposed responses that are in substantial compliance with CCP 2033.220 - except with respect to RFA 3. Plaintiff shall provide a revised response to RFA 3 that complies with CCP 2033.220. When responses are served late, CCP 2033.280(c) makes an award of sanctions "mandatory." As with the motion on interrogatories, given the overlap between motions, the amount of hours requested appears excessive. Sanctions are GRANTED in the amount of $7,635 (10 hours for Mr. Banchero at $645/hour + 3 hours for Ms. Banchero at $395/hour), payable by 11/29. CCP 2033.280, CRC 3.1348(a). Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to sborrowman@gmail.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)
